prompt: I've been learning about large-scale language models recently, and I've decided to train a decoder-only language model on my own. The first step is to prepare pre-training data, so I need your help organizing the pre-training data for llama and gpt-neo into the `ptdata` sheet in `LLM Pre-training Data` spreadsheet. The pre-training data should be sorted in descending order by data size, with columns named `name`, `use in llm` (the value should only include llama and gpt-neo; if used by both models, it should be either gpt-neo or llama), `size` (the numeric value in GB, but do not include "GB" in cell), and `link` in order. The link to huggingface is preferred; if huggingface doesn't have the dataset, we'll try to provide other links.
evaluation_criteria: Correctly identify pre-training datasets for llama and gpt-neo; accurate sizes, correct HuggingFace links, proper sorting and Google Sheets formatting.
output:
The `ptdata` sheet in the `LLM Pre-training Data` spreadsheet (`LLM_Pre-training_Data_ptdata.csv`) has been organized with 24 pre-training datasets for LLaMA and GPT-Neo, sorted by size (GB) descending. Key details:

- **7 LLaMA datasets** (from the LLaMA paper, Touvron et al. 2023): CommonCrawl (3300 GB), C4 (783 GB), GitHub (95 GB), Wikipedia (92 GB), Books (88 GB), plus ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to GPT-Neo.
- **17 GPT-Neo datasets** (from The Pile, Gao et al. 2020): Pile-CC (227 GB), PubMed Central (90 GB), OpenWebText2 (62 GB), ArXiv (56 GB), FreeLaw (51 GB), StackExchange (32 GB), USPTO Backgrounds (22 GB), PubMed Abstracts (19 GB), OpenSubtitles (13 GB), Gutenberg (11 GB), DM Mathematics (7 GB), BookCorpus2 (6 GB), EuroParl (5 GB), HackerNews (4 GB), YoutubeSubtitles (3 GB), PhilPapers (3 GB), NIH ExPorter (2 GB), Ubuntu IRC (1 GB), Enron Emails (1 GB).
- Columns: `name`, `use in llm` (llama or gpt-neo only), `size` (numeric GB, no unit), `link` (HuggingFace preferred).
- Overlapping datasets (Wikipedia, GitHub, Books) assigned to llama; ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to gpt-neo.
finish_summary:
The `ptdata` sheet in the `LLM Pre-training Data` spreadsheet (`LLM_Pre-training_Data_ptdata.csv`) has been organized with 24 pre-training datasets for LLaMA and GPT-Neo, sorted by size (GB) descending. Key details:

- **7 LLaMA datasets** (from the LLaMA paper, Touvron et al. 2023): CommonCrawl (3300 GB), C4 (783 GB), GitHub (95 GB), Wikipedia (92 GB), Books (88 GB), plus ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to GPT-Neo.
- **17 GPT-Neo datasets** (from The Pile, Gao et al. 2020): Pile-CC (227 GB), PubMed Central (90 GB), OpenWebText2 (62 GB), ArXiv (56 GB), FreeLaw (51 GB), StackExchange (32 GB), USPTO Backgrounds (22 GB), PubMed Abstracts (19 GB), OpenSubtitles (13 GB), Gutenberg (11 GB), DM Mathematics (7 GB), BookCorpus2 (6 GB), EuroParl (5 GB), HackerNews (4 GB), YoutubeSubtitles (3 GB), PhilPapers (3 GB), NIH ExPorter (2 GB), Ubuntu IRC (1 GB), Enron Emails (1 GB).
- Columns: `name`, `use in llm` (llama or gpt-neo only), `size` (numeric GB, no unit), `link` (HuggingFace preferred).
- Overlapping datasets (Wikipedia, GitHub, Books) assigned to llama; ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to gpt-neo.

4. Wikipedia - 4.5% of training mix
5. Books - 4.5% of training mix (Bookcorpus + Books3/Book3)
6. ArXiv - 2.5% of training mix
7. StackExchange - 2% of training mix

- Books: 90GB (Books3 + Bookcorpus)

- StackExchange: 78GB

- Wikipedia: https://huggingface.co/datasets/wikimedia/wikipedia → correct
- GitHub: https://huggingface.co/datasets/codeparrot/github-code → reasonable
- Books/Books3: https://huggingface.co/datasets/EliHei2/the_pile_books3 → Books3 specifically
- ArXiv: https://huggingface.co/datasets/EleutherAI/arxiv → might not exist, maybe should be EleutherAI/the_pile or just arxiv
- StackExchange: https://huggingface.co/datasets/EleutherAI/pile → points to The Pile
- All other Pile components: point to EleutherAI/pile → reasonable since they're all parts of The Pile

For the Pile components that don't have separate HuggingFace datasets, pointing to EleutherAI/pile is the best option. Some components do have separate datasets though:
- OpenWebText2: EleutherAI/openwebtext2 ✓
- Books3: EliHei2/the_pile_books3 or TheUltimateTagman/the_pile_books3

Let me finalize the corrected data and create the updated CSV.

input.summary:
The `ptdata` sheet in the `LLM Pre-training Data` spreadsheet (`LLM_Pre-training_Data_ptdata.csv`) has been organized with 24 pre-training datasets for LLaMA and GPT-Neo, sorted by size (GB) descending. Key details:

- **7 LLaMA datasets** (from the LLaMA paper, Touvron et al. 2023): CommonCrawl (3300 GB), C4 (783 GB), GitHub (95 GB), Wikipedia (92 GB), Books (88 GB), plus ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to GPT-Neo.
- **17 GPT-Neo datasets** (from The Pile, Gao et al. 2020): Pile-CC (227 GB), PubMed Central (90 GB), OpenWebText2 (62 GB), ArXiv (56 GB), FreeLaw (51 GB), StackExchange (32 GB), USPTO Backgrounds (22 GB), PubMed Abstracts (19 GB), OpenSubtitles (13 GB), Gutenberg (11 GB), DM Mathematics (7 GB), BookCorpus2 (6 GB), EuroParl (5 GB), HackerNews (4 GB), YoutubeSubtitles (3 GB), PhilPapers (3 GB), NIH ExPorter (2 GB), Ubuntu IRC (1 GB), Enron Emails (1 GB).
- Columns: `name`, `use in llm` (llama or gpt-neo only), `size` (numeric GB, no unit), `link` (HuggingFace preferred).
- Overlapping datasets (Wikipedia, GitHub, Books) assigned to llama; ArXiv and StackExchange assigned to gpt-neo.
